title: [lora_l2.c:252] generate_eui64 uses SHA-256 truncation without domain separation
description:
Contract: generate_eui64 should produce unique 8-byte identifier from hardware ID.
Violation: The function hashes hwid with SHA-256 and takes first 8 bytes (line 295). If any other part of the system also uses SHA-256(hwid)[0:8] for a different purpose, collision could occur. No domain separation tag is included in the hash input.
Impact: Low in current codebase - no other code uses this pattern. However, if future code derives other identifiers from hwid using SHA-256, truncation collision is possible.
Mitigation: Consider using tagged hash: SHA-256('LICHEN-EUI64-v1' || hwid) to provide domain separation. This is a defensive improvement, not a current bug.
close_reason: Added domain separation prefix 'LICHEN-EUI64-v1' to SHA-256 hash input before hwid. Hash now computes SHA-256(prefix || hwid) instead of SHA-256(hwid). This ensures the EUI-64 derivation produces different output than other potential uses of SHA-256 on the same hardware ID. Also added secure_zero for the hash_input buffer in cleanup.
